花池大神神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大神神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居]] '''大神神社'''（おおみわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|花池 (一宮市)|Q127432704|花池}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}を称する。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 * '''{{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主神}}''' （おおものぬしのかみ） *: {{jalink|大神氏|Q11438330}}（三輪氏）の奉斎する神で、{{jalink|大神神社|Q705542}}（{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|桜井市|Q127026}}）の祭神{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 大物主神とは別に、『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』大和国神別 工造条に{{jalink|火明命}}十世孫として見える大美和都禰命を祭神とする説があり、これにより{{jalink|尾張氏|Q11465311}}（火明命の後裔氏族）と当社奉斎氏族との同祖関係が推測される{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|社殿
{{Small|左に本殿、右に幣殿・拝殿。}}]] 創建は不詳。{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''三明神'''、'''三宮明神'''などと呼ばれていた{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。平安中期成立の『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「大神神社 名神大」に比定される{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。社名は、写本によっては「大神々社」「太神社」とも表記される。{{jalink|観応|Q1145053}}元年（{{jalink|1350年|Q6094}}）の『中島長利寄進状』（妙興寺文書）では、大神の地は砥墓（現在の一宮市大和町戸塚）の北東部になるとあり、この記載が名神大社比定の傍証になる{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 {{Quotation|寄進　尾張國中嶋郡内田地事
合貳段者　在所暗水柳副
四至限東'''大神''' 限西阿弥陀寺領 限南砥墓{{Efn|「砥」は石偏に弖と書かれている。}} 限北'''大神'''
（中略）|『中島長利寄進状』観応元年七月二日}} 一方、他の名神大社と異なり『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』臨時祭 名神祭条では当社の記載がないため、神名帳の「名神大」を衍字と見る説もあるが、肯定説では『{{jalink|貞観式|Q11634547}}』や臨時祭式の成立後に当社が名神大社の列に加わったためと推測する{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。また『{{jalink|倭名類聚抄}}』に見える郷名のうちで、当地を中島郡美和郷にあてて「みわ（神/美和）」のつながりを見る説が古くからあるが、『新編 一宮市史』では拝師郷または川埼郷にあてる{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 社伝によると、当地は熱田社（{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065}}）の荘園（熱田庄）で、毎年旧暦7月7日には熱田社に{{jalink|素麺|Q701057}}と{{jalink|ハス|Q16528|蓮}}を奉納したといい、「花池」の地名はその名残とする{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。しかし『{{jalink|尾張国地名考}}』ではこの伝承は否定されており、真偽は不詳{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701}}を奉斎する大和の{{jalink|大神氏|Q11438330}}（三輪氏）一族が来住し祭祀を担ったと見る説や、大美和都禰命を奉斎する{{jalink|尾張氏|Q11465311}}関係氏族が祭祀を担ったと見る説がある{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）5月には{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。現在では尾張国{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}を称し、全国一の宮会に加盟している（ただし一般的には尾張国一宮は{{jalink|真清田神社|Q705916}}とされる『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 中世諸国一宮制研究会編、岩田書院、2000年、pp. 102-113。）。これに関して大神神社側では、真清田神社・大神神社が対の宮であったとし、ともに一宮となったと主張している。現在{{jalink|神職|Q60523}}は常駐しておらず、一宮市内の{{jalink|大神社 (一宮市)|Q11438337|大神社}}の{{jalink|宮司|Q11453500}}が兼務する。 === 神階 === 表記はいずれも「大神名神」。位階は写本により異同がある。 * 正一位（『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』熱田本） * 従一位[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 『神社覈録 上編』]（皇典研究所、1902年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）413コマ。（『尾張国内神名帳』密蔵院本、天文本） * 正二位上{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 『国内神名帳』]}}（文政13年（1830年）転写合本、愛知県図書館「貴重和本デジタルライブラリー」より）6コマ。 （『尾張国内神名帳』国府宮本） === 摂末社 === 現在の摂末社は、次の6社境内説明板。。 * 六所社 * 三島社 * 白山社 * 神明社 * 招魂社 * 素盞社 === 文化財 === === 一宮市指定有形文化財 === * 木造狛犬 1対（彫刻） *: {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}後期、{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12年（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）の作。阿形・吽形の2体からなる。阿形は高さ40センチメートル、吽形は高さ45センチメートルで、いずれも檜材{{jalink|寄木造}}{{Sfn|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年|p=191}}。{{jalink|1982年|Q2436}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}57年）3月1日指定{{Sfn|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年|p=240}}。 === その他 === 神宝として刀剣3振があり、一宮市博物館に保管されている。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|花池 (一宮市)|Q127432704|花池}}2-15-28 ; 交通アクセス * 鉄道 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850|名鉄}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}} {{jalink|妙興寺駅|Q6949401}}下車、徒歩で約5分。 ** 名鉄名古屋本線 {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}下車、徒歩で約20分。 ** {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|東海道線 (名古屋地区)|Q11235139|東海道本線}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}下車、徒歩で約20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=大神神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/808}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）808-809コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=大神神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 23 愛知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=田邊裕|chapter=大神々社|year=1989|title=式内社調査報告 第8巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=一宮の文化財めぐり 増補改訂版|publisher=一宮市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa 尾張の国 一の宮 大神神社] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみわしんしや いちのみやし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:Pages]] [[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおみわしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:一宮|加]] [[Category:need translation]]

花池大神神社 |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|10月10日|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=大神神社|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|鳥居]] '''大神神社'''（おおみわじんじゃ）は、{{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|花池 (一宮市)|Q127432704|花池}}にある{{jalink|神社|Q845945}}。{{jalink|式内社}}（{{jalink|名神大社|Q9610964}}）で、{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}を称する。 === 祭神 === 祭神は次の1柱{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 * '''{{jalink|大物主|Q10936701|大物主神}}''' （おおものぬしのかみ） *: {{jalink|大神氏|Q11438330}}（三輪氏）の奉斎する神で、{{jalink|大神神社|Q705542}}（{{jalink|奈良県|Q131287}}{{jalink|桜井市|Q127026}}）の祭神{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 大物主神とは別に、『{{jalink|新撰姓氏録|Q1045362}}』大和国神別 工造条に{{jalink|火明命}}十世孫として見える大美和都禰命を祭神とする説があり、これにより{{jalink|尾張氏|Q11465311}}（火明命の後裔氏族）と当社奉斎氏族との同祖関係が推測される{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 === 歴史 === [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) shaden.JPG|thumb|220px|right|社殿
{{Small|左に本殿、右に幣殿・拝殿。}}]] 創建は不詳。{{jalink|江戸期}}まで'''三明神'''、'''三宮明神'''などと呼ばれていた{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。平安中期成立の『{{jalink|延喜式神名帳|Q11064932}}』に記載のある{{jalink|尾張国|Q514506}}{{jalink|中島郡|Q780420}}「大神神社 名神大」に比定される{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。社名は、写本によっては「大神々社」「太神社」とも表記される。{{jalink|観応|Q1145053}}元年（{{jalink|1350年|Q6094}}）の『中島長利寄進状』（妙興寺文書）では、大神の地は砥墓（現在の一宮市大和町戸塚）の北東部になるとあり、この記載が名神大社比定の傍証になる{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 {{Quotation|寄進　尾張國中嶋郡内田地事
合貳段者　在所暗水柳副
四至限東'''大神''' 限西阿弥陀寺領 限南砥墓{{Efn|「砥」は石偏に弖と書かれている。}} 限北'''大神'''
（中略）|『中島長利寄進状』観応元年七月二日}} 一方、他の名神大社と異なり『{{jalink|延喜式|Q1342448}}』臨時祭 名神祭条では当社の記載がないため、神名帳の「名神大」を衍字と見る説もあるが、肯定説では『{{jalink|貞観式|Q11634547}}』や臨時祭式の成立後に当社が名神大社の列に加わったためと推測する{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。また『{{jalink|倭名類聚抄}}』に見える郷名のうちで、当地を中島郡美和郷にあてて「みわ（神/美和）」のつながりを見る説が古くからあるが、『新編 一宮市史』では拝師郷または川埼郷にあてる{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 社伝によると、当地は熱田社（{{jalink|熱田神宮|Q482065}}）の荘園（熱田庄）で、毎年旧暦7月7日には熱田社に{{jalink|素麺|Q701057}}と{{jalink|ハス|Q16528|蓮}}を奉納したといい、「花池」の地名はその名残とする{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。しかし『{{jalink|尾張国地名考}}』ではこの伝承は否定されており、真偽は不詳{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 {{jalink|大物主|Q10936701}}を奉斎する大和の{{jalink|大神氏|Q11438330}}（三輪氏）一族が来住し祭祀を担ったと見る説や、大美和都禰命を奉斎する{{jalink|尾張氏|Q11465311}}関係氏族が祭祀を担ったと見る説がある{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}。 {{jalink|明治|Q3125096}}5年（{{jalink|1872年|Q7753}}）5月には{{jalink|近代社格制度|Q712534}}において{{jalink|郷社|Q134917316}}に列した{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}。現在では尾張国{{jalink|一宮|Q1656379}}を称し、全国一の宮会に加盟している（ただし一般的には尾張国一宮は{{jalink|真清田神社|Q705916}}とされる『中世諸国一宮制の基礎的研究』 中世諸国一宮制研究会編、岩田書院、2000年、pp. 102-113。）。これに関して大神神社側では、真清田神社・大神神社が対の宮であったとし、ともに一宮となったと主張している。現在{{jalink|神職|Q60523}}は常駐しておらず、一宮市内の{{jalink|大神社 (一宮市)|Q11438337|大神社}}の{{jalink|宮司|Q11453500}}が兼務する。 === 神階 === 表記はいずれも「大神名神」。位階は写本により異同がある。 * 正一位（『{{jalink|尾張国内神名帳|Q11041830}}』熱田本） * 従一位[{{NDLDC|991014/413}} 『神社覈録 上編』]（皇典研究所、1902年、国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）413コマ。（『尾張国内神名帳』密蔵院本、天文本） * 正二位上{{PDFlink|[https://websv.aichi-pref-library.jp/wahon/pdf/1103283156-001.pdf 『国内神名帳』]}}（文政13年（1830年）転写合本、愛知県図書館「貴重和本デジタルライブラリー」より）6コマ。 （『尾張国内神名帳』国府宮本） === 摂末社 === 現在の摂末社は、次の6社境内説明板。。 * 六所社 * 三島社 * 白山社 * 神明社 * 招魂社 * 素盞社 === 文化財 === === 一宮市指定有形文化財 === * 木造狛犬 1対（彫刻） *: {{jalink|江戸時代|Q184963}}後期、{{jalink|文化 (元号)|Q1373146|文化}}12年（{{jalink|1815年|Q6948}}）の作。阿形・吽形の2体からなる。阿形は高さ40センチメートル、吽形は高さ45センチメートルで、いずれも檜材{{jalink|寄木造}}{{Sfn|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年|p=191}}。{{jalink|1982年|Q2436}}（{{jalink|昭和|Q327567}}57年）3月1日指定{{Sfn|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年|p=240}}。 === その他 === 神宝として刀剣3振があり、一宮市博物館に保管されている。 === 現地情報 === ; 所在地 * {{jalink|愛知県|Q80434}}{{jalink|一宮市|Q270810}}{{jalink|花池 (一宮市)|Q127432704|花池}}2-15-28 ; 交通アクセス * 鉄道 ** {{jalink|名古屋鉄道|Q30850|名鉄}}{{jalink|名鉄名古屋本線|Q1137838|名古屋本線}} {{jalink|妙興寺駅|Q6949401}}下車、徒歩で約5分。 ** 名鉄名古屋本線 {{jalink|名鉄一宮駅|Q2856589}}下車、徒歩で約20分。 ** {{jalink|東海旅客鉄道|Q513679|JR}}{{jalink|東海道線 (名古屋地区)|Q11235139|東海道本線}} {{jalink|尾張一宮駅|Q871495}}下車、徒歩で約20分。 === 脚注 === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 注釈 === {{Notelist}} === 出典 === {{Reflist|2}} === 参考文献 === * 境内説明板 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=明治神社誌料編纂所|author=|year=1912|chapter=大神神社|title={{jalink|府県郷社明治神社誌料|Q11486103}}|publisher=明治神社誌料編纂所|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} ** [{{NDLDC|1088244/808}} 『明治神社誌料 府県郷社 上』]（国立国会図書館デジタルコレクション）808-809コマ参照。 * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=大神神社|title={{jalink|日本歴史地名大系|Q17213509}} 23 愛知県の地名|publisher={{jalink|平凡社|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=式内社研究会|author=田邊裕|chapter=大神々社|year=1989|title=式内社調査報告 第8巻|publisher={{jalink|皇學館大学|Q1797584}}出版部|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=一宮の文化財めぐり 増補改訂版|publisher=一宮市教育委員会|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === 外部リンク === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa 尾張の国 一の宮 大神神社] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみわしんしや いちのみやし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおみわしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:一宮|加]] [[Category:need translation]]

Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Hanaike (Ichinomiya)|Q127432704|Hanaike}} 2-15-28 |位置 = {{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Senior First Rank|Q11123258}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
(styled as) {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{jalink|10th century|Q8052}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{jalink|Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{jalink|Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{jalink|Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{jalink|Masumida Shrine|Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Myōkōji Station|Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite Worship hall |所在地 = {{jalink|Aichi Prefecture|Q80434}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q270810}}{{jalink|Hanaike (Ichinomiya)|Q127432704|Hanaike}} 2-15-28 |位置 = {{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}} |神体 = |社格 = {{jalink|Senior First Rank|Q11123258}}
{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
(styled as) {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{jalink|10th century|Q8052}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{jalink|Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{jalink|Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{jalink|Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{jalink|Masumida Shrine|Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Myōkōji Station|Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみわしんしや いちのみやし}} == Categories == {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
(styled as) {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{jalink|10th century|Q8052}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{jalink|Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{jalink|Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{jalink|Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{jalink|Masumida Shrine|Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Myōkōji Station|Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみわしんしや いちのみやし}} == Categories == {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
(styled as) {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{jalink|10th century|Q8052}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{jalink|Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{jalink|Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{jalink|Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{jalink|Masumida Shrine|Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Myōkōji Station|Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみわしんしや いちのみやし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおみわしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:一宮|加]]

Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{jalink|Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{jalink|Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{jalink|Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{jalink|Masumida Shrine|Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Myōkōji Station|Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{jalink|Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{jalink|Heibonsha|Q11482108}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{jalink|Kōgakkan University|Q1797584}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} {{Normdaten}} {{DEFAULTSORT:おおみわしんしや いちのみやし}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:ichinomiya]] [[Category:Miwa shrines]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Owari Province]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:一宮市の神社]] [[Category:名神大社]] [[Category:尾張国の式内社|名おおみわしんしや]] [[Category:愛知県の旧郷社]] [[Category:三輪神社]] [[Category:一宮|加]][[Category:qq]]

{{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|Myojin-grand}})
(styled as) {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}
former {{jalink|Gōsha|Q134917316}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{jalink|10th century|Q8052}}) |本殿 = {{jalink|Nagare-zukuri|Q210684}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{jalink|October 10|Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{jalink|Shinto shrine|Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{jalink|Shikinaisha}} ({{jalink|Myojin Taisha|Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{jalink|Owari Province|Q514506}}{{jalink|Ichinomiya|Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{jalink|Ōmononushi|Q10936701|Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{jalink|Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{jalink|Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{jalink|Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{jalink|Owari clan|Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{jalink|Edo period}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{jalink|Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{jalink|Engishiki|Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{jalink|Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{jalink|1872|Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{jalink|Gōsha}} under the {{jalink|modern shrine rank system}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{jalink|Masumida Shrine|Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Myōkōji Station|Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{jalink|Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{jalink|Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill||lt=Shikinaisha|UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill||lt=Edo period|UNKNOWN}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{ill||lt=Gōsha|UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill||lt=modern shrine rank system|UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=UNKNOWN|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-grand|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
(styled as) {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{ill|lt=10th century|Q8052|WD=Q8052|1=10th century}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === [[EN:Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]] [[FR:Ōmiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya)]] [[JA:大神神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q546197}} == Japanese content == {{混同|大神社 (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Ōmiwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Owari_Oomiwa_Shrine_haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Hanaike (Ichinomiya)|Q127432704|WD=Q127432704|lt=Hanaike|1=Hanaike}} 2-15-28 |位置 = {{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-grand|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
(styled as) {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{ill|lt=10th century|Q8052|WD=Q8052|1=10th century}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}}
