Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Hanaike (Ichinomiya)|Q127432704|WD=Q127432704|lt=Hanaike|1=Hanaike}} 2-15-28 |位置 = {{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-grand|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
(styled as) {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{ill|lt=10th century|Q8052|WD=Q8052|1=10th century}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Hanaike (Ichinomiya)|Q127432704|WD=Q127432704|lt=Hanaike|1=Hanaike}} 2-15-28 |位置 = {{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-grand|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
(styled as) {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{ill|lt=10th century|Q8052|WD=Q8052|1=10th century}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite [[EN:Ōmiwa Shrine, Ichinomiya]] [[FR:Ōmiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya)]] [[JA:大神神社 (一宮市)]] {{wikidata link|Q546197}} == Japanese content == {{混同|大神社 (Ichinomiya)}} {{神社 |名称 = Ōmiwa Shrine |画像 = [[File:Owari_Oomiwa_Shrine_haiden.jpeg|270px]]
Worship hall |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Aichi Prefecture|Q80434|WD=Q80434|1=Aichi Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q270810|WD=Q270810|1=Ichinomiya, Aichi}}{{ill|lt=Hanaike (Ichinomiya)|Q127432704|WD=Q127432704|lt=Hanaike|1=Hanaike}} 2-15-28 |位置 = {{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|region:JP-23_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}} |神体 = |社格 = {{ill|lt=Senior First Rank|Q11123258|WD=Q11123258|1=Senior First Rank}}
{{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=Myojin-grand|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
(styled as) {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}
former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{ill|lt=10th century|Q8052|WD=Q8052|1=10th century}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} former {{ill|lt=Gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Unknown (before {{ill|lt=10th century|Q8052|WD=Q8052|1=10th century}}) |本殿 = {{ill|lt=Nagare-zukuri|Q210684|WD=Q210684|1=Nagare-zukuri}} |別名 = Sanmyōjin
Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === {{脚注ヘルプ}} === Notes === {{Notelist}} === References === {{Reflist|2}} === Bibliography === * Precinct explanation board * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|author=|year=1912|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō|Q11486103|WD=Q11486103|1=Fuken Gōsha Meiji Jinja Shiryō}}|publisher=Meiji Jinja Shiryō Compilation Office|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|明治神社誌料|1912年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1981|chapter=Ōmiwa Shrine|title={{ill|lt=Nihon Rekishi Chimei Taikei|Q17213509|WD=Q17213509|1=Japanese Historical Topography Series}} 23 Place Names of Aichi Prefecture|publisher={{ill|lt=Heibonsha|Q11482108|WD=Q11482108|1=Heibonsha}}|isbn=4582490239|ref={{Harvid|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=Shikinaisha Study Group|author=Yutaka Tanabe|chapter=Ōkamigami-sha|year=1989|title=Shikinaisha Survey Report Vol.8|publisher={{ill|lt=Kōgakkan University|Q1797584|WD=Q1797584|1=Kogakkan University}} Press|page=|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|大神々社（式内社）|1989年}}}} * {{Cite book|ja|editor=|author=|year=1999|chapter=|title=Tour of Cultural Properties of Ichinomiya (revised)|publisher=Ichinomiya City Board of Education|isbn=|ref={{Harvid|一宮の文化財めぐり|1999年}}}} === External links === * [https://oomiwa.wixsite.com/hanaike-oomiwa Owari no Kuni Ichinomiya Ōmiwa Shrine] * {{Commonscat-inline}} {{神道 横}} It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Sannomiya Myōjin
Ōmiwa Myōjin
Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes === Hanaike Ōmiwa Shrine |札所等 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=October 10|Q2921|WD=Q2921|1=October 10}} |神事 = |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Aichi|label=Ōmiwa Shrine|coordinates= {{coord2|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E|}}|position=right|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=13|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|coord={{coord|35|17|29.5|N|136|47|35.7|E}}}} }} [[File:Omiwa-jinja (Ichinomiya) torii.JPG|thumb|220px|right|Torii]] '''Ōmiwa Shrine''' (おおみわじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Hanaike, Ichinomiya, Aichi Prefecture. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=Myojin Taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}), and styles itself as {{ill|lt=Owari Province|Q514506|WD=Q514506|1=Owari Province}}{{ill|lt=Ichinomiya|Q1656379|WD=Q1656379|1=Ichinomiya}}. === Enshrined deity === The enshrined deity is the following{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. * '''{{ill|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi|Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|WD=Q10936701|lt=Ōmononushi-no-Kami|1=Ōmononushi}}''' *: A deity worshipped by the {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa clan|Q11438330|WD=Q11438330|1=Miwa clan}} (Miwa clan), and the deity of {{ill|lt=Ōmiwa Shrine|Q705542|WD=Q705542|1=Ōmiwa Shrine}} (Sakurai, Nara){{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Apart from Ōmononushi, a theory identifies the deity as Ōmiwatsune-no-Mikoto (seen in the '{{ill|lt=Shinsen Shōjiroku|Q1045362|WD=Q1045362|1=Shinsen Shōjiroku}}'), suggesting common ancestry between the {{ill|lt=Owari clan|Q11465311|WD=Q11465311|1=Owari clan}} and this shrine’s worship clan{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. === History === Founding unknown. Until the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Edo period|1=Edo period|WD=Q184963}} it was called '''Sanmyōjin''' or '''Sannomiya Myōjin'''{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. It is identified with “Ōmiwa Shrine, Myojin-grand” in Nakashima District, Owari Province, in the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki Jinmyōchō|Q11064932|WD=Q11064932|1=Engishiki Jinmyōchō}}'{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. Depending on manuscript, name appears as “Ōkami-gami-sha” or “Taisha.” Unlike other Myojin-grand shrines, it is not listed in the Myojin-festival section of the '{{ill|lt=Engishiki|Q1342448|WD=Q1342448|1=Engishiki}}' temporary rites, leading to a view that “Myojin-grand” in Jinmyōchō is an interpolation. The affirmative view infers it joined Myojin-grand rank after completion of '{{ill|lt=Jōgan-shiki|Q11634547|WD=Q11634547|1=Jōgan-shiki}}' and temporary ritual codes{{Sfn|大神神社（平凡社）|1981年}}. In Meiji 5 ({{ill|lt=1872|Q7753|WD=Q7753|1=1872}}) May, it was ranked {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Gōsha|1=UNKNOWN}} under the {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=modern shrine rank system|1=UNKNOWN}}{{Sfn|明治神社誌料|1912年}}. Today it claims Owari Ichinomiya and belongs to the National Ichinomiya Association (though generally Owari Ichinomiya is {{ill|lt=Masumida Shrine|Q705916|WD=Q705916|1=Masumida Shrine}}). The shrine side argues Masumida and Ōmiwa were paired shrines and both became Ichinomiya. === Divine ranks === All written as “Ōmiwa Myōjin.” Rank varies by manuscript. * Senior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Atsuta text) * Junior First Rank ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Mizō-in and Tenbun texts) * Senior Second Rank Upper ('Owari no Kuni Jinmyōchō' Kōnomiya text) === Sessha and massha === Current six: * Rokusho-sha * Mishima-sha * Hakusan-sha * Shinmei-sha * Shōkon-sha * Susano-sha === Cultural properties === === Ichinomiya City-designated Tangible Cultural Property === * One pair of wooden komainu (sculpture) === Other === As shrine treasures, there are 3 swords kept at Ichinomiya City Museum. === Local information === ; Address * Hanaike 2-15-28, Ichinomiya, Aichi ; Access * Rail ** About 5 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Myōkōji Station|Q6949401|WD=Q6949401|1=Myōkōji Station}} on Meitetsu Nagoya Main Line. ** About 20 minutes on foot from {{ill|lt=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station|Q2856589|WD=Q2856589|1=Meitetsu Ichinomiya Station}}. ** About 20 minutes on foot from JR {{ill|lt=Owari-Ichinomiya Station|Q871495|WD=Q871495|1=Owari-Ichinomiya Station}}. === Footnotes ===
